title: DWT Corpus Scenario
description: A single scenario in the waste-movement corpus. Each entry describes one path through the BA's flow chart, tagged against the seven-axis taxonomy, and optionally accompanied by an ordered list of API steps that replays the scenario against the extended Digital Waste Tracking API. The corpus is regenerated by the Import app whenever the source flow chart changes; steps may be either generated by the Import app (corpus level — template shape) or authored by hand (curated level — concrete fixtures).

id.description: Stable identifier of the form C-NNNN, where NNNN is zero-padded. IDs are assigned at extraction time and SHOULD remain stable across re-extractions where the underlying scenario shape is unchanged.

name.description: Human-readable label summarising the scenario. Generated by the Import app from the axis tuple, e.g. 'Carrier, real-time collection, accept-all, no cycles'.

axes.description: The seven-axis taxonomy. Every value is mechanically derived from the decision points the path traverses.

initiator.description: Who creates the movement and mints the Movement ID.

collectionRecording.description: When and how the collection event is recorded. 'na' applies when no collection event occurs (producer-tracking-only paths).

receiptOutcome.description: What the receiver decides at the gate. acceptPart variants split the load into two parallel flows; the variant indicates which side this scenario tracks. 'na' applies when no receipt event occurs.

receiptRecording.description: When and how the receipt event is recorded. 'na' applies when no receipt event occurs (full rejection, or producer-tracking-only paths).

finalState.description: Terminal state of the path: Accepted-at-Receiver, reconciled-by-Carrier, observed-by-Producer, or Waste-Returned-with-Producer.

cycles.description: Loop edges traversed. The corpus permits four arrangements: none, exactly one of the three single loops, or specifically the combination of multi-collection and multi-drop-off. Rejection-retry combinations are excluded — at most one loop is traversed when rejection-retry is present.

compliance.description: Derived: any deferred-and-not-reconciled trajectory is non-compliant. Stored explicitly to make filtering and validation possible.

path.description: Ordered list of node labels from the Mural flow chart, as walked from start to terminal node. This is the raw extraction output and the source-of-record for the axis tags.

steps.description: Optional ordered list of API call steps that replays the scenario against the extended Digital Waste Tracking API. Two fill levels are supported, both conforming to the same step shape: a template form (field names mapped to '<required>' / '<optional>' placeholder markers, suitable for the mechanically-generated corpus level), and a concrete form (fixture references with real data, suitable for curated scenarios). Step order is significant and must follow the order of events implied by the path.

source.description: Provenance metadata, populated by the Import app. Lets us trace a corpus entry back to a specific extraction run.

muralBoardId.description: Identifier of the Mural board the scenario was extracted from.

extractedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the extraction run that produced this entry.

nodeCount.description: Number of nodes in the source diagram at extraction time. Useful for spotting structural changes between runs.

edgeCount.description: Number of edges in the source diagram at extraction time.

step.description: A single API call within a scenario. The 'event' value matches an OpenAPI operationId from the extended DWT API specification exactly; the 'actor' identifies the role making the call. 'request' and 'expect' are both optional — corpus-level steps may carry only 'event' and 'actor'; richer levels of fill add request shape and expected outcome.

actor.description: The role making the API call. Mapped from the kind of node in the Mural chart that this step corresponds to.

event.description: The OpenAPI operationId being invoked. The enum lists every operation in the current API specification; if the spec adds a new operation, this enum must be widened in the same change.

request.description: Optional. Two forms are accepted: an inline template object (field names mapped to '<required>' or '<optional>' placeholder markers) for corpus-level mechanical generation; or a $ref pointing to a fixture file with concrete data for curated scenarios.

oneOf.description: Template form: keys are field names from the request body, values are placeholder markers describing whether the field is required or optional in this step.
Fixture reference form: a single $ref pointing to a JSON or YAML fixture file with concrete request data.

expect.description: Optional. Expected outcome of the API call.

status.description: Expected HTTP status code, e.g. 201 for successful creation, 200 for successful retrieval, 400 for validation failure.

returns.description: Identifiers and projections expected in the response body. Values must match those documented in the OpenAPI spec for the operation.
